metadata.description: SOC and Platform Power Rail Knowledge Base

soc_rails.name: VCCCORE
soc_rails.description: Main CPU cores (Performance cores), core logic
soc_rails.debug_hints: Except for Browsing/Catapult or ADK browsing KPI, all Power KPIs should be running on E-Cores. P-Cores should be bare minimal close to 0.

soc_rails.name: VCCST
soc_rails.description: Always-on domains, retention SRAM, deep AON logic
soc_rails.debug_hints: Depends on PC10 residency. "AGGREGATE-SUBSYSTEM" value which is reported in nsec, if is less than 1msec will cause lower PC10 residency. We should have AGGREGATE-SUBSYSTEM residency reported in LTR Snoop Histogram in Socwatch should be lesser in <1msec range. more the residency in <1msec range more VccST power.
socwatch_metrics: LTR Snoop Histogram: AGGREGATE-SUBSYSTEM bucket residency

soc_rails.name: VCCGT
soc_rails.debug_hints: Graphics: RC0 residency or GFX P-state frequency.

soc_rails.name: VCCSA
soc_rails.description: System Agent, fabric, memory controller, display engine, NPU, IPU
soc_rails.debug_hints:
Regression here can be detected by checking Residency, Frequency & Bandwidth of each IPs connected to this rail. Need to look at below info from Socwatch csv log.
1. Memory: MEMSS Freq/SAGV Freq & Total Memory Bandwidth
2. IPU: IS/PS Frequency For WCL, there is NO IPU, so ignore. 
3. NPU: NPU D0 residency, NPU Frequency, NPU Bandwidth
4. Media: Media-C0 Residency, Media Frequency
5. Display: Cdclock from ETL or Display P-State & Display VC1 bandwidth from Socwatch

soc_rails.name: VCC_LP_ECORE
soc_rails.debug_hints: If this Regresses, mostly Software issue. To debug, start with ACPI C-State C0, if this is regressed confirmed Software regression i.e OS/App is asking for more work. Check for Overall Platform Activity in Socwatch. Upon Confirmation rely more on ETL traces as this is mostly Software.

soc_rails.name: VDD2_CPU
soc_rails.debug_hints: This Regresses when Total Memory Bandwidth regresses.

soc_rails.name: VCCPRIM_VNNAON
soc_rails.description: Always-on infrastructure, fuse sense, platform logic
soc_rails.debug_hints: If this regresses, check for WLAN/BT LTR value & Aggregated LTR. If they look same then look for LTR Snoop Histogram to check which Ips are requesting <1msec. They are concerning thing for power. Look for WLAN Module power from Platform to double confirm the regression due to High WLAN activity.

soc_rails.name: VCCPRIM_IO
soc_rails.description: General IO, PLLs, DTS, CEP, platform IO
soc_rails.debug_hints: Platform IO transfers or SSD IO transfer. SSD Reads or Writes will impact PRIM_IO. Check for SSD component power on Platform to double confirm the high SSD actviity.

soc_rails.name: VCCPRIM_1P8
soc_rails.description: Analog blocks, PLLs, some IO, display PHY

soc_rails.name: VCCPRIM_1P8_FLTR
soc_rails.description: Filtered analog supply, sensitive analog Ips

soc_rails.name: VCCPRIM_3P3
soc_rails.description: Legacy IO, eSPI, SPI, some platform logic

soc_rails.name: VCCPRIM_UCIE
soc_rails.debug_hints: Compute Die to PCD die connectivity.

soc_rails.name: P_SOC
soc_rails.description: Summation of all SOC power rails. Primary one to be tracked.
soc_rails.debug_hints: SOC = Sum of all SOC rails.

platform_rails.name: P_MEMORY
platform_rails.description: Memory Module power on RVP. Impacted by Memory bandwidth & Freq of operation. Depends on no. of ranks, capacity, no. of Channels & Memory Technology(LP5 vs DDR5)

platform_rails.name: P_DISPLAY
platform_rails.description: Panel electronics power on RVP. Power Consumption by Timing Controller(Tcon) & PSR SRAM. Impacted by variations in refresh rate & PSR memory usage. Depends on Tcon vendor, LRR feature supports& Resolutions.

platform_rails.name: P_BACKLIGHT
platform_rails.description: Backlight power on RVP. Power Consumption from LEDs & drivers. Impacted by brightness level of Screen, controlled through PWM module of PCH. depends on physical size of Display and Luminance capacity.

platform_rails.name: P_WLAN
platform_rails.description: WLAN module power on RVP. Power Consumption by Radio, Antennas. Depends on Tx or Rx from these Radios.

platform_rails.name: P_SSD
platform_rails.description: NVMe SSD component power on RVP. Power consumption from Component side SSD controller, DRAM cache, Flash memory chips from x4 PCIe lane. Impacted by Reads & writes to SSD, IO Throughput, PCIe link states. Depends on NVMe type Gen Gen4/Gen5), Capacity, NVMe vendor, Flash chip type (SLC/MLC/QLC)
platform_rails.debug_hints: SSDs are connected to PCIe controller A or B.

platform_rails.name: P_VBATA
platform_rails.description: Battery input power which represents Total System power inclusive of VR losses and sum of all above rails
